reasoning: The daily average temperature in Bordeaux in late October is typically around 13-14°C, whereas in late January it is around 6-7°C. The difference in means is about 7°C. The standard deviation of the daily average temperature for a specific day is roughly 3.5-4°C. Therefore, the standard deviation of the difference between an October day and a January day is roughly 5.3°C. Assuming a normal distribution, the probability of the January day being warmer than the October day is approximately P(Z > 7/5.3), which corresponds to P(Z > 1.32) ≈ 0.09. Without specific weather forecasts available for the exact dates in these specific future years, the climatological base rate provides the most robust estimate.
